heading: Notice, consultations, and reports
label: 4204 Notice, consultations, and reports

children.heading: Notice, consultations, and reports before negotiation
children.label: a Notice, consultations, and reports before negotiation
A
children.text: provide, at least 90 calendar days before initiating negotiations with a country, written notice to Congress of the President’s intention to enter into the negotiations with that country and set forth in the notice the date on which the President intends to initiate those negotiations, the specific United States objectives for the negotiations with that country, and whether the President intends to seek an agreement, or changes to an existing agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: before and after submission of the notice, consult regarding the negotiations with the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate, such other committees of the House and Senate as the President deems appropriate, and the House Advisory Group on Negotiations and the Senate Advisory Group on Negotiations convened under ;section 4203(c) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: upon the request of a majority of the members of either the House Advisory Group on Negotiations or the Senate Advisory Group on Negotiations convened under , meet with the requesting congressional advisory group before initiating the negotiations or at any other time concerning the negotiations; andsection 4203(c) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: after consulting with the Committee on Ways and Means and the Committee on Finance, and at least 30 calendar days before initiating negotiations with a country, publish on a publicly available Internet website of the Office of the United States Trade Representative, and regularly update thereafter, a detailed and comprehensive summary of the specific objectives with respect to the negotiations, and a description of how the agreement, if successfully concluded, will further those objectives and benefit the United States.

children.heading: Assessment and consultations following assessment
children.label: A Assessment and consultations following assessment
i
children.text: assess whether United States tariffs on agricultural products that were bound under the Uruguay Round Agreements are lower than the tariffs bound by that country;

children.label: ii
children.text: consider whether the tariff levels bound and applied throughout the world with respect to imports from the United States are higher than United States tariffs and whether the negotiation provides an opportunity to address any such disparity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: consult with the Committee on Ways and Means and the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate concerning the results of the assessment, whether it is appropriate for the United States to agree to further tariff reductions based on the conclusions reached in the assessment, and how all applicable negotiating objectives will be met.

children.heading: Special consultations on import sensitive products
children.label: B Special consultations on import sensitive products
I
children.text: identify those agricultural products subject to tariff rate quotas on , and agricultural products subject to tariff reductions by the United States as a result of the Uruguay Round Agreements, for which the rate of duty was reduced on , to a rate which was not less than 97.5 percent of the rate of duty that applied to such article on ;June 29, 2015January 1, 1995December 31, 1994

children.label: aa
children.text: whether any further tariff reductions on the products identified under subclause (I) should be appropriate, taking into account the impact of any such tariff reduction on the United States industry producing the product concerned;

children.label: bb
children.text: whether the products so identified face unjustified sanitary or phytosanitary restrictions, including those not based on scientific principles in contravention of the Uruguay Round Agreements; and

children.label: cc
children.text: whether the countries participating in the negotiations maintain export subsidies or other programs, policies, or practices that distort world trade in such products and the impact of such programs, policies, and practices on United States producers of the products;

children.label: III
children.text: request that the International Trade Commission prepare an assessment of the probable economic effects of any such tariff reduction on the United States industry producing the product concerned and on the United States economy as a whole; and

children.label: IV
children.text: upon complying with subclauses (I), (II), and (III), notify the Committee on Ways and Means and the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate of those products identified under subclause (I) for which the Trade Representative intends to seek tariff liberalization in the negotiations and the reasons for seeking such tariff liberalization.

children.label: I
children.text: the United States Trade Representative identifies any additional agricultural product described in clause (i)(I) for tariff reductions which were not the subject of a notification under clause (i)(IV), or

children.label: II
children.text: any additional agricultural product described in clause (i)(I) is the subject of a request for tariff reductions by a party to the negotiations,

children.heading: Negotiations regarding the fishing industry
children.label: 3 Negotiations regarding the fishing industry
children.text: Before initiating, or continuing, negotiations that directly relate to fish or shellfish trade with any country, the President shall consult with the Committee on Ways and Means and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Finance and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate, and shall keep the Committees apprised of the negotiations on an ongoing and timely basis.

children.label: A
children.text: assess whether United States tariffs on textile and apparel products that were bound under the Uruguay Round Agreements are lower than the tariffs bound by that country and whether the negotiation provides an opportunity to address any such disparity; and

children.label: B
children.text: consult with the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate concerning the results of the assessment, whether it is appropriate for the United States to agree to further tariff reductions based on the conclusions reached in the assessment, and how all applicable negotiating objectives will be met.

children.heading: Adherence to existing international trade and investment agreement obligations
children.label: 5 Adherence to existing international trade and investment agreement obligations
children.text: In determining whether to enter into negotiations with a particular country, the President shall take into account the extent to which that country has implemented, or has accelerated the implementation of, its international trade and investment commitments to the United States, including pursuant to the WTO Agreement.

children.heading: Consultation with Congress before entry into agreement
children.label: b Consultation with Congress before entry into agreement
A
children.text: the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: each other committee of the House and the Senate, and each joint committee of Congress, which has jurisdiction over legislation involving subject matters which would be affected by the trade agreement; and

children.label: C
children.text: the House Advisory Group on Negotiations and the Senate Advisory Group on Negotiations convened under .section 4203(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the nature of the agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: how and to what extent the agreement will achieve the applicable purposes, policies, priorities, and objectives of this chapter; and

children.label: C
children.text: the implementation of the agreement under , including the general effect of the agreement on existing laws.section 4205 of this title

children.heading: Report regarding United States trade remedy laws
children.label: 3 Report regarding United States trade remedy laws

children.heading: Changes in certain trade laws
children.label: A Changes in certain trade laws
i
children.text: the range of proposals advanced in the negotiations with respect to that agreement, that may be in the final agreement, and that could require amendments to title VII of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( et seq.) or to chapter 1 of title II of the Trade Act of 1974 ( et seq.); and19 U.S.C. 167119 U.S.C. 2251

children.label: ii
children.text: how these proposals relate to the objectives described in .section 4201(b)(17) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: no other resolution with respect to that report has previously been reported in that House of Congress by the Committee on Ways and Means or the Committee on Finance, as the case may be, pursuant to those procedures; and

children.label: II
children.text: no procedural disapproval resolution under introduced with respect to a trade agreement entered into pursuant to the negotiations to which the report under subparagraph (A) relates has previously been reported in that House of Congress by the Committee on Ways and Means or the Committee on Finance, as the case may be.section 4205(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “resolution” means only a resolution of either House of Congress, the matter after the resolving clause of which is as follows: “That the ____ finds that the proposed changes to United States trade remedy laws contained in the report of the President transmitted to Congress on ____ under section 105(b)(3) of the Bipartisan Congressional Trade Priorities and Accountability Act of 2015 with respect to ____, are inconsistent with the negotiating objectives described in section 102(b)(17) of that Act.”, with the first blank space being filled with the name of the resolving House of Congress, the second blank space being filled with the appropriate date of the report, and the third blank space being filled with the name of the country or countries involved.

children.label: I
children.text: may be introduced by any Member of the House;

children.label: II
children.text: shall be referred to the Committee on Ways and Means and, in addition, to the Committee on Rules; and

children.label: III
children.text: may not be amended by either Committee.

children.label: I
children.text: may be introduced by any Member of the Senate;

children.label: II
children.text: shall be referred to the Committee on Finance; and

children.label: III
children.text: may not be amended.

children.label: v
children.text: It is not in order for the House of Representatives to consider any resolution that is not reported by the Committee on Ways and Means and, in addition, by the Committee on Rules.

children.label: vi
children.text: It is not in order for the Senate to consider any resolution that is not reported by the Committee on Finance.

children.label: vii
children.text: The provisions of subsections (d) and (e) of section 152 of the Trade Act of 1974 () (relating to floor consideration of certain resolutions in the House and Senate) shall apply to resolutions.19 U.S.C. 2192

children.heading: Advisory committee reports
children.label: 4 Advisory committee reports
children.text: The report required under section 135(e)(1) of the Trade Act of 1974 () regarding any trade agreement entered into under subsection (a) or (b) of shall be provided to the President, Congress, and the United States Trade Representative not later than 30 days after the date on which the President notifies Congress under section 4202(a)(2) or 4205(a)(1)(A) of this title of the intention of the President to enter into the agreement.19 U.S.C. 2155(e)(1)section 4202 of this title

children.heading: Submission of information to Commission
children.label: 1 Submission of information to Commission
children.text: The President, not later than 90 calendar days before the day on which the President enters into a trade agreement under , shall provide the International Trade Commission (referred to in this subsection as the “Commission”) with the details of the agreement as it exists at that time and request the Commission to prepare and submit an assessment of the agreement as described in paragraph (2). Between the time the President makes the request under this paragraph and the time the Commission submits the assessment, the President shall keep the Commission current with respect to the details of the agreement.section 4202(b) of this title

children.heading: Assessment
children.label: 2 Assessment
children.text: Not later than 105 calendar days after the President enters into a trade agreement under , the Commission shall submit to the President and Congress a report assessing the likely impact of the agreement on the United States economy as a whole and on specific industry sectors, including the impact the agreement will have on the gross domestic product, exports and imports, aggregate employment and employment opportunities, the production, employment, and competitive position of industries likely to be significantly affected by the agreement, and the interests of United States consumers.section 4202(b) of this title

children.heading: Review of empirical literature
children.label: 3 Review of empirical literature
children.text: In preparing the assessment under paragraph (2), the Commission shall review available economic assessments regarding the agreement, including literature regarding any substantially equivalent proposed agreement, and shall provide in its assessment a description of the analyses used and conclusions drawn in such literature, and a discussion of areas of consensus and divergence between the various analyses and conclusions, including those of the Commission regarding the agreement.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 4 Public availability
children.text: The President shall make each assessment under paragraph (2) available to the public.

children.heading: Reports submitted to committees with agreement
children.label: d Reports submitted to committees with agreement
A
children.text: conduct environmental reviews of future trade and investment agreements, consistent with Executive Order No. 13141 (64 Fed. Reg. 63169), dated , and its relevant guidelines; andNovember 16, 1999

children.label: B
children.text: submit a report on those reviews and on the content and operation of consultative mechanisms established pursuant to to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate at the time the President submits to Congress a copy of the final legal text of an agreement pursuant to .section 4201(c) of this titlesection 4205(a)(1)(E) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: review the impact of future trade agreements on United States employment, including labor markets, modeled after Executive Order No. 13141 (64 Fed. Reg. 63169) to the extent appropriate in establishing procedures and criteria; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit a report on such reviews to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate at the time the President submits to Congress a copy of the final legal text of an agreement pursuant to .section 4205(a)(1)(E) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a meaningful labor rights report of the country, or countries, with respect to which the President is negotiating; and

children.label: B
children.text: a description of any provisions that would require changes to the labor laws and labor practices of the United States.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 4 Public availability
children.text: The President shall make all reports required under this subsection available to the public.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: At the time the President submits to Congress a copy of the final legal text of an agreement pursuant to , the President shall also submit to Congress a plan for implementing and enforcing the agreement.section 4205(a)(1)(E) of this title

children.heading: Border personnel requirements
children.label: A Border personnel requirements
children.text: A description of additional personnel required at border entry points, including a list of additional customs and agricultural inspectors.

children.heading: Agency staffing requirements
children.label: B Agency staffing requirements
children.text: A description of additional personnel required by Federal agencies responsible for monitoring and implementing the trade agreement, including personnel required by the Office of the United States Trade Representative, the Department of Commerce, the Department of Agriculture (including additional personnel required to implement sanitary and phytosanitary measures in order to obtain market access for United States exports), the Department of Homeland Security, the Department of the Treasury, and such other agencies as may be necessary.

children.heading: Customs infrastructure requirements
children.label: C Customs infrastructure requirements
children.text: A description of the additional equipment and facilities needed by U.S. Customs and Border Protection.

children.heading: Impact on State and local governments
children.label: D Impact on State and local governments
children.text: A description of the impact the trade agreement will have on State and local governments as a result of increases in trade.

children.heading: Cost analysis
children.label: E Cost analysis
children.text: An analysis of the costs associated with each of the items listed in subparagraphs (A) through (D).

children.heading: Budget submission
children.label: 3 Budget submission
children.text: The President shall include a request for the resources necessary to support the plan required by paragraph (1) in the first budget of the President submitted to Congress under after the date of the submission of the plan.section 1105(a) of title 31

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 4 Public availability
children.text: The President shall make the plan required under this subsection available to the public.

children.heading: Report on penalties
children.label: 1 Report on penalties
children.text: Not later than one year after the imposition by the United States of a penalty or remedy permitted by a trade agreement to which this chapter applies, the President shall submit to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate a report on the effectiveness of the penalty or remedy applied under United States law in enforcing United States rights under the trade agreement, which shall address whether the penalty or remedy was effective in changing the behavior of the targeted party and whether the penalty or remedy had any adverse impact on parties or interests not party to the dispute.

children.heading: Report on impact of trade promotion authority
children.label: 2 Report on impact of trade promotion authority
children.text: Not later than one year after , and not later than 5 years thereafter, the United States International Trade Commission shall submit to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate a report on the economic impact on the United States of all trade agreements with respect to which Congress has enacted an implementing bill under trade authorities procedures since .June 29, 2015January 1, 1984

children.label: A
children.text: The United States Trade Representative shall consult with the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate after acceptance of a petition for review or taking an enforcement action in regard to an obligation under a trade agreement, including a labor or environmental obligation. During such consultations, the United States Trade Representative shall describe the matter, including the basis for such action and the application of any relevant legal obligations.

children.label: B
children.text: As part of the report required pursuant to section 163 of the Trade Act of 1974 (), the President shall report annually to Congress on enforcement actions taken pursuant to a trade agreement to which the United States is a party, as well as on any public reports issued by Federal agencies on enforcement matters relating to a trade agreement.19 U.S.C. 2213

children.heading: Additional coordination with Members
children.label: g Additional coordination with Members
children.text: Any Member of the House of Representatives may submit to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and any Member of the Senate may submit to the Committee on Finance of the Senate the views of that Member on any matter relevant to a proposed trade agreement, and the relevant Committee shall receive those views for consideration.
